text: This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil. And I'm Georgie. Neil, I'm going to play you two sound recordings and I want you to tell me how you feel when you hear them.
zh: 我是尼尔。我是乔琪。尼尔,我要给你放两段录音,我想让你告诉我听到它们时的感受。

text: OK. First this… …and now this.
zh: 好的。先听这段……再听这段。

text: Oh, well, that first clip made me feel really relaxed.
zh: 哦,第一段录音让我感觉非常放松。

text: But the second one, oh, it's horrible. I felt quite stressed out, actually.
zh: 但第二段,哦,太可怕了。说实话,我感觉压力很大。

text: Oh no! Well, this little experiment shows how much we are affected by the noise around us.
zh: 天哪!这个小实验说明了我们周围的噪音对我们的影响有多大。

text: And that's a problem if you're one of the four billion people around the world who live in cities.
zh: 如果你是全球四十亿住在城市里的人之一,这就是个问题了。

text: Yes, whether it's the rattle of train tracks, honking car horns or the roar of airplanes planes overhead,
zh: 是的,不管是火车轨道的咔嗒声、汽车喇叭的鸣笛声,还是头顶飞机的轰鸣声,

text: modern cities are full of noise and this can have serious effects.
zh: 现代城市里充满了噪音,而这会带来严重的影响。

text: The World Health Organisation reports that by 2050, around 2 .5 billion people will experience hearing loss
zh: 世界卫生组织报告称,到2050年,大约有25亿人将会经历听力损失,

text: and exposure to loud noise has been linked to stress and anxiety.
zh: 而暴露在噪音中也与压力和焦虑有关。

text: In this episode, we'll be hearing about ways to make our cities quieter, more relaxing places, using some useful new words and phrases.
zh: 在这一期节目中,我们将通过一些实用的新词汇和短语,聊聊如何让城市变得更安静、更让人放松。

text: And remember, you'll find all the vocabulary along with a quiz and worksheet on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 记住,你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到所有词汇,还有测验和练习题。

text: But now I have a question for you, Georgie. Sound is measured in units called decibels, but how many decibels would it be
zh: 不过现在我有个问题要问你,乔琪。声音是用一种叫“分贝”的单位来测量的,那么如果我小声说话,

text: if I whispered, a whisper is speaking like this?
zh: 也就是像这样耳语,会是多少分贝呢?

text: Is it a 3 decibels, b 13 decibels or c 30 decibels?
zh: 是a. 3分贝,b. 13分贝,还是c. 30分贝?

text: That's really hard. I'll go for b 13 decibels.
zh: 这个真的很难。我选b,13分贝。

text: Well, we'll find out the answer at the end of the programme.
zh: 好,我们会在节目最后揭晓答案。

text: Let's start in one of the noisiest cities in the world, Mumbai in India,
zh: 我们先来看看世界上最吵闹的城市之一——印度孟买,

text: where daytime noise levels regularly hit 80 decibels,
zh: 那里白天的噪音水平经常达到80分贝,

text: which is over 20 decibels higher than World Health Organisation recommendations.
zh: 比世界卫生组织建议的水平高出20多分贝。

text: Listen to Mumbai native Chavi Sachdev share her experiences
zh: 让我们听听孟买本地人查维·萨切德夫分享她的经历,

text: of living in this noisy city with BBC World Service programme People Fixing the World.
zh: 讲述她在这个喧闹城市生活的感受,内容来自BBC世界服务节目《人们在解决世界难题》。

text: I mean, India in general is just really loud. We are a loud people.
zh: 我是说,印度整体上就是很吵。我们是个大嗓门的民族。

text: We dress loud, we talk loud, we celebrate loud, we watch movies loud.
zh: 我们穿得张扬,说话大声,庆祝热闹,看电影也开得很大声。

text: When people ask me what I miss about living abroad, I tell them it's the quiet because after 12 years of living in the US and
zh: 当人们问我在国外生活时最想念什么,我告诉他们是安静,因为在美国和

text: Europe, when I got back, I realised I couldn't handle it anymore.
zh: 欧洲生活了12年后,回国时我意识到自己已经受不了这种嘈杂了。

text: I'd gotten used to lower levels of noise. Chavi says Indians talk loud, celebrate loud and dress loud.
zh: 我已经习惯了较低的噪音水平。查维说印度人说话大声、庆祝热闹、穿着也很张扬。

text: To dress loud means to wear clothes with bright colours and large bold patterns.
zh: “穿得张扬”是指穿颜色鲜艳、图案大胆的衣服。

text: When Chavi returned to India after living overseas, she says she couldn't handle the noise.
zh: 查维说,在国外生活后回到印度,她无法忍受那里的噪音。

text: If you can't handle something, you find it difficult to deal or cope with.
zh: 如果你“无法忍受”某事,就是说你觉得应对或处理这件事很困难。

text: So what can be done to tackle noise in cities like Mumbai?
zh: 那么,像孟买这样的城市,该如何应对噪音问题呢?

text: One interesting approach involves using sound itself to make things feel quieter.
zh: 一种有趣的方法是利用声音本身,让环境听起来更安静。

text: Soundscaping is the idea of adding certain sounds to busy public spaces so that they sound and feel more relaxing.
zh: “声景设计”就是在繁忙的公共空间中加入某些声音,让人感觉更放松、更舒适。

text: Sound artist Charles Montembeault lives near the Parc de Madelaineau, a busy city park in Montreal, Canada.
zh: 声音艺术家查尔斯·蒙特博住在加拿大蒙特利尔一个繁忙的城市公园——马德兰公园附近。

text: Charles was inspired to recreate the ocean sounds he heard on holiday in the Magdalen Islands, in his city park back home.
zh: 查尔斯曾在马格达伦群岛度假时听到海浪声,深受启发,于是想在家乡的城市公园里重现这种海洋之声。

text: On BBC World Service programme People Fixing the World, he tells reporter Natasha Fernandes
zh: 在BBC世界服务节目《人们在解决世界难题》中,他向记者娜塔莎·费尔南德斯讲述,

text: that these sounds included ocean waves and some unusual sounding sand.
zh: 这些声音包括海浪声,以及一种奇特的沙子声音。

text: The sand and the Magdalen Island can sing. When you walk into the sound firmly, the sand makes some squeaky sounds.
zh: 马格达伦群岛的沙子会“唱歌”。当你使劲踩上去时,沙子会发出吱吱的声音。

text: So that's one sound that is kind of funny, kind of special.
zh: 这是一种挺有趣、也挺特别的声音。

text: Inside this small park, surrounded by busy roads,
zh: 在这个被繁忙道路环绕的小公园里,

text: Charles and his team set up an immersive sound installation in the form of speakers planted in flower beds.
zh: 查尔斯和他的团队打造了一套沉浸式声音装置,把扬声器藏在花坛里。

text: So the installation was really to make this park a little quieter
zh: 这个装置的目的其实是想让这个公园显得更安静一些,

text: by adding some some quiet noises. So
zh: 方法是加入一些安静的声音。

text: it's counterintuitive, but you can make
zh: 这听起来有点反常识,

text: a park less noisy by adding some calmer sounds.
zh: 但你确实可以通过加入更平静的声音,让公园显得不那么吵。

text: The sand on the Magdalen Islands is squeaky. It makes a high -pitched sound called a squeak when stepped on.
zh: 马格达伦群岛的沙子会“吱吱作响”。踩上去时会发出一种高音调的声音,叫做“squeak”。

text: Charles played recordings of this sand, ocean waves and other relaxing sounds through speakers hidden in the park.
zh: 查尔斯通过藏在公园里的扬声器,播放了这种沙子声、海浪声和其他令人放松的声音。

text: He created an art installation, a work of art designed to give people an interactive experience.
zh: 他创作了一件“装置艺术”,也就是一种旨在带给人们互动体验的艺术作品。

text: By adding more relaxing sounds, Charles' installation actually made the park feel quieter.
zh: 通过加入更多让人放松的声音,查尔斯的装置反而让公园显得更安静了。

text: It's an example of something that's counter -intuitive, meaning it happens differently from how you would expect.
zh: 这就是一个“反直觉”的例子,意思是事情的发生方式与你预期的不同。

text: What a great idea! And similar soundscapes are now helping city -dwellers relax in other cities too, including Tokyo and Barcelona.
zh: 真是个好主意!类似的声景设计如今也在东京、巴塞罗那等其他城市,帮助城市居民放松身心。

text: Now, Neil, you asked me a question about a very quiet sound, sound
zh: 好了,尼尔,你之前问我一个关于非常轻的声音的问题——

text: – a whisper. So, are you going to reveal the correct answer?
zh: 耳语。那么,你要公布正确答案了吗?

text: I asked you how loud a whisper is.
zh: 我问你耳语有多少分贝。

text: Yes, and I said 13 decibels. Well, 13 is an unlucky number and you are wrong. Ugh!
zh: 是的,我说是13分贝。嗯,13是个不吉利的数字,而你答错了。啊!

text: It was actually 30. OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned, starting with decibel, the unit for measuring sound.
zh: 正确答案其实是30。好了,我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇,先从“分贝”开始,这是测量声音的单位。

text: Someone who dresses loud likes to wear clothes with bright colours and bold designs.
zh: “穿得张扬”的人喜欢穿颜色鲜艳、设计大胆的衣服。

text: If you can't handle something, you find it difficult to deal with.
zh: 如果你“无法忍受”某事,就是说你觉得处理这件事很困难。

text: An art installation is an artwork designed to create an interactive experience in a certain space.
zh: “装置艺术”是指专为在某个空间内营造互动体验而设计的艺术作品。

text: The adjective squeaky describes things which make a high -pitched sound, like a mouse.
zh: 形容词“squeaky”用来形容发出高音调声音的事物,比如老鼠的叫声。

text: Squeak, squeak! And finally, if you call something counterintuitive, you mean it happens differently from how you would expect.
zh: 吱吱!最后,如果你说某事“反直觉”,意思是它的发生方式与你预期的不同。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but if you'd like to hear more about this and many other trending topics,
zh: 我们的六分钟又到时间了,不过如果你想了解更多关于这个话题以及其他热门话题的内容,

text: you'll find more episodes and learning activities on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到更多节目和学习活动。

text: See you again soon, but for now, it's Goodbye.
zh: 我们很快再见,不过现在,先说“再见”。

text: 6 Minute English From bbclearningenglish .com
zh: 《六分钟英语》,来自bbclearningenglish.com
